# F2 · Functional Programming

> The Functional Programming chapter: pure functions, persistent data, higher-order functions, folds, closures, and composition — a guided path through nine modules ending in a data-pipeline lab.

Chapter map for LLMs/agents. Hub: [/elixir/functional](/elixir/functional). Links are clean root-relative URLs; this file lists every lesson in the chapter in course order.

## Lessons

- [Pure functions & side effects — F2.01](/elixir/functional/pure): What purity buys and how to keep it: same input gives the same output, what counts as a side effect, and the functional core / imperative shell that isolates effects at…
- [Immutability & persistent data — F2.02](/elixir/functional/persistence): Why copying is cheap: immutable values, structural sharing in lists and maps, and the memory cost of a full copy versus rebuilding only what changed.
- [Higher-order functions — F2.03](/elixir/functional/higher-order): Functions as values: passing a function into Enum.map, the differing signatures map / filter / reduce / sort_by expect, and a factory that returns a function carrying a…
- [Recursion patterns & tail calls — F2.04](/elixir/functional/recursion): Recursion as the functional way to repeat: the call stack, tail calls and accumulators for constant stack space, and the patterns that recur — across three deep-dive…
  - [The shape of recursion — F2.04.1](/elixir/functional/recursion/shape): Base case and recursive case, and the call stack growing then unwinding as a body-recursive function runs to a result.
  - [Tail calls & accumulators — F2.04.2](/elixir/functional/recursion/tail-calls): Body versus tail recursion, the accumulator pattern, and how a tail call reuses the stack frame to run in constant space.
  - [Recursion patterns — F2.04.3](/elixir/functional/recursion/patterns): sum, length, reverse, map, and filter written recursively — and why each one is a fold over the list.
- [map / filter / reduce (folds) — F2.05](/elixir/functional/folds): reduce as the universal fold: how the accumulator threads through a list, how swapping the combiner changes the result, and how map and filter are reduce with a list…
  - [map — F2.05.1](/elixir/functional/folds/map): Transforming every element with a function: one-to-one output, length and order preserved, and why chained maps fuse into one.
  - [filter — F2.05.2](/elixir/functional/folds/filter): Keeping elements that pass a predicate: filter and its inverse reject, and filtering then mapping as a pipeline.
  - [reduce — F2.05.3](/elixir/functional/folds/reduce): The general fold: accumulators of any shape — numbers, lists, maps — and building a frequency map step by step.
  - [Advanced folds — F2.05.4](/elixir/functional/folds/advanced): The Enum toolkit as folds with extra structure: scan as a running fold, plus map_reduce, flat_map, group_by, and frequencies.
- [Closures & partial application — F2.06](/elixir/functional/closures): A closure is a function plus the environment it captured. Building specialised functions by capturing a value, partial application, and the & capture operator.
  - [Capturing the environment — F2.06.1](/elixir/functional/closures/environment): What a closure captures and when: the value at definition time, immutability, lexical scope, and capturing several variables at once.
  - [The capture operator — F2.06.2](/elixir/functional/closures/capture): The & shorthand for anonymous functions: positional placeholders &1 and &2, and capturing named functions with &Module.fun/arity.
  - [Partial application & currying — F2.06.3](/elixir/functional/closures/currying): Fixing arguments to specialise a function, and building curried functions by hand — applying arguments one at a time.
- [Algebraic data types — F2.07](/elixir/functional/adt): The algebra of types: product types hold several values at once and their counts multiply; sum types are one shape or another and their counts add; pattern matching…
  - [Product types — F2.07.1](/elixir/functional/adt/product): Tuples and structs: bundling fields by position or by name, immutable struct update, and why a product type's inhabitants multiply.
  - [Sum types — F2.07.2](/elixir/functional/adt/sum): Tagged tuples and variants: a value is one shape or another, the atom tag discriminates, and the inhabitants add — including the {:ok, _} | {:error, _} idiom.
  - [Pattern matching on data — F2.07.3](/elixir/functional/adt/matching): Taking algebraic data apart: destructuring products to bind fields, and dispatching on sum variants with case and function heads.
- [Composition & pipelines — F2.08](/elixir/functional/composition): Building programs by combining functions: composing two so one feeds the next, the pipe operator that threads a value left to right, and pipelines of map, filter, and…
  - [Function composition — F2.08.1](/elixir/functional/composition/compose): Composing functions by hand — f after g — why the order matters, and chaining three together.
  - [The pipe operator — F2.08.2](/elixir/functional/composition/pipe): The |> operator: threading a value as the first argument, reading left to right instead of inside out, and passing extra arguments.
  - [Building pipelines — F2.08.3](/elixir/functional/composition/pipeline): Composing map, filter, and reduce into a pipeline over a dataset, watching the value transform at each stage.
- [The data-pipeline lab — F2.09](/elixir/functional/pipeline-lab): The F2 capstone: compose filter, map, sort, and reduce stages over a dataset, watch the rows transform at each stage, and read the idiomatic Elixir pipeline the…
